figures.caption: Figure 2. World Machine train process, when all protocols are used. It is structured in loops, with the addition of a “segment loop”, created to iterate through the segments. Each loop has code callback points. The actions are organized by the protocols that implement them.
Figure 3. Loss of the optimizer of the models during the training of the protocol experiment.
Figure 4. Model variations metrics in tasks

Figure 5. Model variations external state inference samples for four sequences in the validation dataset. The predictions of only one model for each variation are shown. The middle vertical bar indicates the middle of the sequence.
Figure 7. Correlation between task performance across variations
Figure 8. Impact of each training variable on task performance
Figure 9. Training variables impact on the sum of train epoch duration. Red corresponds to a bad impact on epoch duration.
Figure 10. Divergence probability conditioned by training variable

Table 1. Training variable relation with hyperparameters. For the “Sequence Breaking”, “Short Time Recall - Future” and “Short Time Recall - Past” domains, only one variable can be active. The “BASE” variable refers for the model trained without any variable in a domain. “M” and “S” refers to the sensorial dimension “Measurement” and “State on the core input”, respectively.
